title: The U.S. Just Added $2.2 Trillion In Debt — Here's Where It's Going
description:
📊 Federal Debt just risen 6.1% year-over-year to $37.6 trillion.

In this episode of The Money Map, we break down what's really happening with federal debt, how it connects to federal funds rate, 10-year treasury yield, gdp growth rate, and what it means for ordinary Americans.

All data sourced from FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) — the same data the Fed uses.

Timestamps:
0:00 — The Number
0:20 — What's Happening
0:50 — The Full Chart
1:30 — Connected Indicators
2:20 — What This Means For You
3:10 — The Takeaway

🔔 Subscribe for weekly data-driven economic analysis.
